summary: Joel Spolsky responds to Paul Boutin's article about struggling with remote desktop assistance software, which took most of an hour to configure. Joel recommends Copilot (Fog Creek's own product) as a simpler alternative that is secure by default, works through firewalls, and takes only minutes to set up.
key_points: Remote desktop tools shouldn't require an hour of installation and configuration
Good software should be secure out of the box without complex setup
Products that 'just work' through firewalls and NAT are superior to those requiring network configuration
money_quotes: Secure out of the box, works through all firewalls, and never takes more than a couple of minutes to get working.
key_insight: Software that requires extensive configuration to accomplish a basic task represents a design failure — the best tools just work out of the box.
